card_description: Heraclitus (Greek: "Glory of Hera") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Heraclitus is pre-socratic philosopher of flux and logos. PKD frequently invokes Heraclitus on change and the hidden logos. Appears in 6 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Heraclitus. Context: "Dick proposes a "Light Body" theory of immortality: a 2,000-year-old collective "Bioplasmic" entity (the fellowship of Christ) re-enters human hosts through the 3rd eye/pineal gland."
